params.text:
How to respond in the moment

How you react when someone first complains sets the tone for everything that follows. A calm, warm response can turn a frustrated person into someone who feels genuinely heard. The core skills are simple but powerful.

  • Listen properly — give the person your full attention and let them finish.
  • Take it seriously — even if it seems minor to you, it matters to them.
  • Stay calm and non-defensive — do not argue, make excuses or interrupt.
  • Apologise sincerely that they have had a poor experience — saying sorry is not admitting legal fault.
  • Don’t take it personally — the person is unhappy with a situation, not attacking you.
  • Reassure them that their concern will be taken forward and acted on.

The power of a good apology

Many complaints escalate simply because no one said sorry. An apology acknowledges the person’s experience and shows respect. It is not an admission of liability, and it should never be withheld out of fear. Being honest and open when things go wrong links directly to the duty of candour, which we return to later.

params.taskDescription: Fill in the key skills for handling a complaint well. Drag each word to the right gap.
params.textField: When someone complains, first *listen* carefully and let them finish. Stay *calm* and do not become defensive. *Apologise* sincerely for their poor experience, remembering this is not an admission of fault. Try not to take it *personally*, and *reassure* the person that their concern will be acted on.
